steps.title: Identify the sentence boundary problem
steps.content: The left side of the blank ends with an independent clause (“The Kyzylkum Desert in central Asia is a semiarid desert”) followed by the word “for.” The right side continues with “a total area of about 300,000 km²,” and then a second independent clause: “it is also one of the largest deserts of any type.”

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule for independent clauses
steps.content: A period properly separates two independent clauses into two sentences. Choice D places a period after “example,” creating the first sentence: “The Kyzylkum Desert in central Asia is a semiarid desert, for example.” Then a second sentence: “With a total area of about 300,000 km², it is also one of the largest deserts of any type.” Choices A, B, and C try to connect both independent clauses in one sentence using only commas and transitional words, which creates a comma splice. That matches choice D — D is correct.
